svg.title: Best GNS3 Marketplace open-source tools for networking and cybersecurity learning
svg.desc: A structured diagram grouping GNS3 appliances by category: network OS, security, monitoring, routing, and attack/defense labs.
svg.text: Click any category to learn more

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about VyOS and FRRouting for GNS3 networking labs — what can I practice with them?')
sendPrompt('What is VyOS and what networking protocols can I learn with it in GNS3?')
sendPrompt('What is FRRouting (FRR) and how do I use it in GNS3 to simulate a full routing lab?')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about OPNsense and pfSense for GNS3 firewall labs')
sendPrompt('What is OPNsense and what can I learn from it in a GNS3 lab?')
sendPrompt('How is pfSense CE different from OPNsense for GNS3 learning labs?')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about Security Onion and ntopng for network monitoring in GNS3 labs')
sendPrompt('What is Security Onion and how do I deploy it in GNS3 to practice threat detection?')
sendPrompt('How can I use ntopng in GNS3 to learn network traffic analysis?')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about Kali Linux and Metasploitable for GNS3 penetration testing labs')
sendPrompt('How do I use Kali Linux as a GNS3 node for penetration testing practice?')
sendPrompt('What is Metasploitable and how do I use it as a vulnerable target in GNS3?')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about Alpine Linux and Ubuntu Docker nodes for simulating servers in GNS3')
sendPrompt('Why is Alpine Linux a great lightweight endpoint node for GNS3 labs?')
sendPrompt('How do I use Ubuntu Server as a GNS3 Docker appliance for simulating web and DNS servers?')

svg.text: • Use Docker appliances — lighter than QEMU VMs
• Link Security Onion to a SPAN/mirror port
• Add a cloud node to reach your real network
Click any box for deeper guidance on that tool or lab stack
